program.name: Telstra Graduate Program
program.open_date_raw: Applications for the 2027 program will open March 2026.
streams: Corporate pathways (Finance and Business, and HR)
program.salary_text: The page says you will be given a competitive salary, but it does not list an amount.
program.rotation_text: During the 14-month period you will be allocated into Chapters/Operative groups and assigned to missions/projects to develop capabilities in chosen pathways. The page also says the program includes a month-long induction at the start.

eligibility.work_rights: You must be eligible to work in Australia. International students with a valid visa that enables them to work in Australia without any work restrictions can apply to technical pathways only.
eligibility.graduation_window: You must have completed a minimum of an undergraduate degree at an Australian University within the last three years before the program starts in February 2027 (i.e. graduating between 2024, 2025 and 2026).
minimum_requirements: You must have completed a minimum of an undergraduate degree at an Australian University within the last three years before the program starts in February 2027 (i.e. graduating between 2024, 2025 and 2026).
You must be eligible to work in Australia.
International students with a valid visa that enables them to work in Australia without any work restrictions are eligible to apply to technical pathways only.

stages.name: Submit application
stages.details: Fill out a short online application form to help determine if you are eligible, and upload your resume and most recent academic transcript.

stages.name: Video interview and online assessment (HireVue)
stages.details: Complete a video interview and an online assessment using HireVue.

stages.name: Final interview (virtual)
stages.details: This has three parts: a graduate ambassador Q&A session, a case study pitch (3 to 5 minutes), and a behavioural interview.

stages.name: Checks
stages.details: Telstra conducts checks such as a security or background check before progressing you to the final stage.

stages.name: Offer
stages.details: If you are successful, Telstra will call you with the outcome. If you narrowly miss out, they may keep you in their talent pool in case roles open later in the year.

tips: For the HireVue video interview: make sure you have the right equipment (phone/PC/laptop, working microphone and camera), use a quiet space, and check your internet connection.
Use the chance to practice before recording your answers.
If you make a mistake, you can re-record your answer (they say you have three attempts for each question).
Do research on Telstra (they suggest reading about Telstra’s strategy, purpose, and values, and reviewing the role description for your pathway).
Set up your camera to avoid strong backlighting and check what is visible in your background.

exec_themes: Shift from the completed T25 program to the new “Connected Future 30” strategy, with a stronger focus on core connectivity and “radical” innovation in how connectivity is delivered and sold.
Network leadership and capacity investment (including using AI in network operations and improving reliability), positioned as a core competitive advantage.
Customer experience improvement, including using AI and digital tools to understand customers better and proactively resolve issues.
Efficiency and cost reduction / tight cost control, alongside disciplined capital management.
Repositioning how value is captured from the network (moving beyond selling raw bandwidth toward selling higher-value connectivity features like prioritisation, scalability, and security).
Portfolio emphasis on infrastructure-style assets and businesses (digital infrastructure, fibre, satellite ground stations) as a growth platform, with ongoing focus on returns (e.g., ROIC).
Enterprise-business turnaround/reset actions after underperformance, with acknowledgement that improvement will take time.
commercial_context.profit_engine: Telstra’s profit engine is earning recurring revenue from connectivity services (especially mobile) and monetising infrastructure assets, supported by cost reduction, disciplined capital management, and improving returns on invested capital. This includes an explicit push to capture more value by selling differentiated connectivity outcomes (e.g., prioritised connectivity, scalable bandwidth, and added security) rather than only selling bandwidth.
commercial_context.esg: Telstra frames “operating sustainably” as an enabler of its Connected Future 30 strategy. In its FY25 Annual Report, Telstra reports climate targets and progress (including scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions reductions, renewable energy enabling, and a 2050 net-zero ambition). It also discloses a notable climate-claims change: it stopped claiming to be a carbon neutral organisation from the start of FY25 (including stopping the purchase of carbon credits to offset operational emissions), and it references human rights/modern slavery governance through its policies and Modern Slavery Act Statement.
commercial_context.recent_pivot: Telstra describes FY25 as the completion of its T25 strategy and a pivot to the new “Connected Future 30” strategy (announced 27 May 2025). This pivot “doubles down” on connectivity, aims to reinvent how Telstra captures value from its network (from selling bandwidth to selling value), and increases focus on efficiency/competitiveness and digital infrastructure expansion (e.g., fibre and satellite ground stations).

notes: Program duration is explicitly stated as 14 months.
Program start timing is stated as beginning February 2027, and application opening timing is stated as March 2026, but an exact day is not provided.
Pathways are listed on the program page. Locations are not explicitly listed on the extracted pages.
The graduation window and work-rights rules are explicitly stated, including the international-student restriction to technical pathways.
Discipline requirements are not explicitly listed on the extracted pages.
The student and graduate recruitment stages (application, HireVue, final interview with three parts, checks, and offers) are explicitly described on Telstra’s pages.
This summary is based on Telstra investor pages and official reports/transcripts located on telstra.com.au only.

The Annual Report and investor transcripts clearly state the strategy shift (T25 completion to Connected Future 30) and several operational priorities (customer experience, efficiency/cost, network leadership).

Some classic ‘headwinds’ (e.g., competitive pressure, regulation, cyber risk, macro conditions) are mentioned as general risk factors in forward-looking statements, but they are not presented as a concise ‘headwinds’ list in the extracted excerpts, so headwinds is left as null rather than inferred.
ESG content is based on explicit climate targets/progress and disclosures about ceasing carbon-neutral claims; broader ESG pillars (e.g., detailed social metrics) are not fully extracted here.
